questions.question: An electric dipole coinsides on Z-axis and its mid point is on origin of the co-ordinate system. The electric field at an axial point at a distance $z$ from origin is $\vec{E}(z)$ and electric field at an equatorial point at a distance $y$ from origin is $\vec{E}(y)$. Here $z = y >> a$, So $\frac{|\vec{E}(z)|}{|\vec{E}(y)|} = ...............$

An electric field is spread uniformly in Y-axis. Consider point A as origin point. The co-ordinates of point B are equal to $(0, 2)\text{ m}$. The co-ordinates of point C are $(2, 0)\text{ m}$. At points A, B and C, electric potentials are $V_A$, $V_B$ and $V_C$ respectively. From the following options which is correct?
To increase the charge on the plates of a capacitor means

options.A: to decrease the potential difference between the plates
options.B: to decrease the capacitance of the capacitor
options.C: to increase the capacitance of the capacitor
options.D: to increase the potential difference between the plates

questions.question: Work done in placing a charge of $8 \times 10^{-18}\text{ C}$ on a capacitor of capacitance $800\text{ microfarad}$ is
If 20 J of work has to be done to move an electric charge of 4 C from a point, where potential is 10 volt to another point, where potential is V volt, find the value of V.

Eight charges, each of magnitude q are placed at the vertices of a cube placed in vacuum. Electric potential at the centre of the cube due to this system of charges is ..... ($\epsilon_0$ is permittivity of vacuum and a is length of each side of the cube)
64 small drops of water having same charge and same radius are combined to form one big drop. The ratio of capacitance of big drop to small drop is
Equivalent capacitance between X and Y point in the given figure is [Figure]
The potential energy of system of two equal negative point charges of $2 \mu\text{C}$ each held $1\text{m}$ apart in air is ............... . $(k = 9 \times 10^9 \text{ SI unit})$

Consider three concentric shells of metal A, B and C are having radii $a$, $b$ and $c$ respectively as shown in the figure ($a < b < c$). Their surface charge densities are $\sigma$, $-\sigma$ and $\sigma$ respectively. Calculate the electric potential on the surface of shell A. [Figure]

A parallel plate capacitor having air as dielectric medium is charged by a potential difference of $V$ volt. After disconnecting the battery, the distance between the plates of the capacitor is increased using an insulated handle. As a result, potential difference between the plates ...........

Two capacitors of capacitance $2\mu\text{F}$ and $4\mu\text{F}$ respectively are connected in series. The combination is connected across a potential difference of $10\text{ V}$. The ratio of energies stored by capacitors will be .........
A hollow metal sphere of radius $10\text{ cm}$ is charged such that the potential on its surface becomes $80\text{ volt}$. The potential at the centre of the sphere is ....

Charges $5\text{ }\mu\text{C}$ and $10\text{ }\mu\text{C}$ are placed $1\text{ m}$ apart. Work done to bring these charges at a distance $0.5\text{ m}$ from each other is ....... $(K = 9 \times 10^9\text{ S.I.})$
The unit of physical quantity obtained by the line integral of electric field is ...

Two identical thin rings each of radius $R \text{ m}$ are kept on the same axis at a distance of $R \text{ m}$ apart. If the charges on them are $10 \text{ C}$ and $5 \text{ C}$ respectively, Calculate the work done in moving charge $q \text{ C}$ from the center of one ring to that of another.
$Q$ amount of electric charge is present on the surface of a sphere having radius $R$. Then electrical potential energy of this system is ________.
One moving electron when comes closer to other stationary electron, then its kinetic energy and potential energy respectively ________ and ________.

Charges $1\text{ }\mu\text{C}$ are placed at each of the four corners of a square of side $2\sqrt{2}\text{ m}$. The potential at the point of intersection of the diagonals is ________. $(K = 9 \times 10^9\text{ SI unit})$ [Diagram]

27 identical drops of mercury are charged simultaneously with the same potential of $10\text{ Volt}$. Assuming the drop to be spherical, if all the charged drops are made to combine to form one large drop, then its potential will be ________ $\text{Volt}$.
The dimensional formula of capacitance is ____. Take Q as the dimension formula of charge.

A uniform electric field is prevailing in X - direction in certain region. The co-ordinates of points $P, Q$, and $R$ are (0, 0), (2, 0) and (0, 2) respectively. Which of the following alternatives is true for the potentials at these points?
An electric dipole of dipole moment $P$ is placed parallel to the uniform electric field of intensity $\vec{E}$. On rotating it through 180°, the amount of work done is _______.
On the axis and on the equator of an electric dipole for all points ____

options.A: On the axis $V = 0$ and on equator $V \neq 0$
options.D: On the axis $V \neq 0$ and on equator $V = 0$

questions.question: In the figure [Diagram] area of each plate is $A$ and the distance between consecutive plates is as shown in the figure. What is the effective capacitance between points A & B.
A moving positive charge approaches a negative charge. What will happen to the potential energy of the system?
The dimensional formula of Polarization P is .......
If relative permittivity for any substance is $80$ then its electric susceptibility is ......

$2\text{ F}$ capacitor is connected with $50\text{ V}$ supply & $3\text{ F}$ capacitor is connected with $100\text{ V}$ supply. Now after removing battery if two plates of same type of charges are placed to form new capacitor then potential difference is ............. $\text{V}$.
Which of the following option gives the Dimensional Formula of Electrical Potential?
Find the equivalent capacitance between two points A & B, for given figure (electric circuit) [Capacitance of each capacitor is $C = 3\mu\text{F}$] [Diagram]
Which of the following option is the pair of polar molecules?
How will you connect 4 (four) capacitors, each of capacitance $4\mu\text{F}$ for having equivalent capacitance $1.6\mu\text{F}$?

options.A: Two in parallel and two in series
options.D: Three in parallel and one in series

questions.question: A slab of material of dielectric constant $3$ has the same area as the plates of a parallel plate capacitor but has a thickness $(\frac{3}{4})d$, where $d$ is the separation of the plates. What is the Electrical potential difference between the plates, when the slab is inserted between the plates? Initial electrical potential difference $V_0$.

A molecule of a substance has a permanent electric dipole moment of magnitude $10^{-29}\text{ cm}$. $2\text{ mole}$ of this substance is polarised (at low temperature) by applying a strong electrostatic field of magnitude $10^6\text{ Vm}^{-1}$. What should be potential energy of its? [1 mole of the substance contains $6 \times 10^{23}\text{ molecules}$]
The potential at the point B in the given figure is ________ V. [Figure]
A charge Q is placed at the centre of circle of radius 10 cm. Find the work done in moving a charge q between any two points lying on the arc of this circle.
The dielectric strength of air is ________.
For an electric dipole an angle between $\vec{E}$ and $\vec{P}$ at a point on the equatorial plane is ________.
If an electron is accelerated by a potential difference of $2.5\text{V}$ it would gain energy of ________. (Take charge of electron $1 \times 10^{-19}\text{C}$)
A radius of spherical charged shell is $10\text{ cm}$ and electric potential on its surface is $100\text{ V}$, then the potential at $2\text{ cm}$ from the centre of the shell will be ________.

A parallel plate capacitor with air between the plates has a capacitance of $4\text{ pF}$. If the distance between the plates is reduced by half and the space between them is filled with a substance of dielectric constant $6$ then the value of capacitance will be ________.
As shown in figure charges $+q, +q, -q$ and $-q$ are placed on the vertices of square, each side length is $2l$. The electric potential at mid-point 'A' of charges $+q$ and $+q$ is ________. [Figure]

Charge $1.6 \times 10^{-7}\text{C}$ are distributed uniformly over the surface of spherical conductor of radius R. The ratio of electric potential inside the spherical conductor to the electric field on the surface is ________.
The potential difference between two plates of parallel plate capacitor is 2V. As shown in figure electrons are placed at point P and Q. So [Figure]

options.A: Electric forces acting on both the electrons are same.
options.B: Electric force acting on the electron at point P is greater than the electron at point Q.
options.C: Electric force acting on the electron at point P is less than the electron at point Q.
options.D: Electric forces acting on both the electrons are zero.
